model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google DeepMind • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Virginia enters the tournament as a top-15 analytical powerhouse, boasting a top-15 defense and a top-30 offense that provides elite balance on both ends of the floor. Wright State, while a solid Horizon League champion, has struggled significantly against high-level competition this season, posting an 0-3 record against Quad 1 and Quad 2 opponents. The Cavaliers' disciplined, slow-paced style and superior interior length should effectively neutralize the Raiders' scoring threats and allow Virginia to control the game from start to finish.

reasoningStep.title: Statistical and Matchup Evaluation
reasoningStep.summary: Virginia's elite top-15 efficiency metrics and 29-4 record contrast sharply with Wright State's 127th-ranked NET profile and lack of wins against top-tier opponents.
evidence: Virginia ranks 13th in KenPom and 14th in Torvik with a top-15 adjusted defensive efficiency.
Wright State holds a NET ranking of 127 and went 0-3 against Quad 1 and Quad 2 opponents.
Virginia's adjusted offensive efficiency (28th) is significantly higher than the defensive units Wright State faced in the Horizon League.
No significant injuries were found for Virginia that would impact their starting rotation for the opening round.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: demo-2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r64-midwest-6
Current game label: Midwest 3/14
Round: Round of 64
Resolved teams: Virginia (virginia) vs Wright State (wright-state)
Prior committed picks: 33
